preview_text:
The model favors USA at 66% to win, with Bosnia and Herzegovina offered only a 9% chance. The draw probability sits at 25%, suggesting a competitive fixture despite the asymmetry in win chances.

The spread between the favorite and underdog is substantial, but the draw's presence at nearly one-quarter probability indicates the match is unlikely to be a blowout. Bosnia and Herzegovina enters as clear underdogs yet retains a non-trivial path to an upset or stalemate. The W/D/L distribution implies a contest where USA control remains likely but neither side is prohibitive.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes, with gaps of 4 percentage points or smaller on USA win and draw probabilities. Polymarket prices show similar alignment. The three sources converge on a USA-favored but genuinely competitive match, with no material disagreement worth flagging for the dashboard.
